heading: Trade Remedy Assistance Office
label: 1339 Trade Remedy Assistance Office
notes: References in TextThe Trade Act of 1974, referred to in subsec. (c)(2)(A) to (C), is , , . Chapters 1, 2, and 3 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974 are classified generally to parts 1 (§ 2251 et seq.), 2 (§ 2271 et seq.), and 3 (§ 2341 et seq.) of subchapter II of chapter 12 of this title, respectively. Chapter 1 of title III of the Trade Act of 1974 is classified generally to subchapter III (§ 2411 et seq.) of chapter 12 of this title. children.text: remedies and benefits available under the trade laws, and
the petition and application procedures, and the appropriate filing dates, with respect to such remedies and benefits.

children.heading: Procedural assistance by Office and other agencies
children.label: b Procedural assistance by Office and other agencies

children.text: to prepare and file petitions and applications (other than those which, in the opinion of the Office, are frivolous); and
to seek to obtain the remedies and benefits available under the trade laws, including any administrative review or administrative appeal thereunder.

The term “eligible small business” means any business concern which, in the agency’s judgment, due to its small size, has neither adequate internal resources nor financial ability to obtain qualified outside assistance in preparing and filing petitions and applications for remedies and benefits under trade laws. In determining whether a business concern is an “eligible small business”, the agency may consult with the Small Business Administration, and shall consult with any other agency that has provided assistance under subsection (b) to that business concern. An agency decision regarding whether a business concern is an eligible small business for purposes of this section is not reviewable by any other agency or by any court.

children.label: A
children.text: chapter 1 of title II of the Trade Act of 1974 ( et seq., relating to injury caused by import competition);19 U.S.C. 2251

children.text: chapters 2 and 3 of such title II [ et seq., 2341 et seq.] (relating to adjustment assistance for workers and firms);19 U.S.C. 2271

children.label: C
children.text: chapter 1 of title III of the Trade Act of 1974 ( et seq., relating to relief from foreign import restrictions and export subsidies);19 U.S.C. 2411

children.label: D
children.text: subtitle IV of this chapter (relating to the imposition of countervailing duties and antidumping duties);

children.label: E
children.text: (relating to the safeguarding of national security); andsection 1862 of this title
